questions.text: A line segment joining a vertex of a triangle to the mid-point of its opposite side is called a:
questions.explanation: The median connects a vertex to the midpoint of the opposite side, dividing the side into two equal parts.

questions.text: How many medians can a triangle have?
questions.explanation: A triangle has three vertices and three sides, hence it can have three medians.

questions.text: The perpendicular line segment from a vertex of a triangle to its opposite side is called an:
questions.explanation: An altitude represents the height of the triangle from a specific vertex.

questions.text: An exterior angle of a triangle is equal to the sum of its interior ________ angles.
questions.explanation: The Exterior Angle Property states that an exterior angle equals the sum of its two interior opposite angles.

questions.text: In a triangle, the sum of the measures of the three interior angles is:
questions.explanation: This is known as the Angle Sum Property of a triangle.

questions.text: If two angles of a triangle are 60° and 40°, find the third angle.
A triangle in which all three sides are of equal length is called:
questions.explanation: In an equilateral triangle, all sides and all angles (60° each) are equal.

questions.text: A triangle with two equal sides is called an:
questions.explanation: In an isosceles triangle, the angles opposite to the equal sides are also equal.

questions.text: The sum of the lengths of any two sides of a triangle is ________ the third side.
questions.explanation: This is the Triangle Inequality Property; if not met, a triangle cannot be formed.

questions.text: Can a triangle have sides of length 2 cm, 3 cm, and 5 cm?
questions.explanation: 2 + 3 is not greater than 5 (it is equal), so no triangle can be formed.

questions.text: In a right-angled triangle, the side opposite to the right angle is called:
questions.explanation: The hypotenuse is the longest side in a right-angled triangle.

questions.text: Pythagoras property states that in a right-angled triangle:
questions.explanation: The square of the hypotenuse equals the sum of the squares of the other two sides.

questions.text: Find the hypotenuse of a right triangle whose legs are 3 cm and 4 cm.
If the exterior angle of a triangle is 110° and one interior opposite angle is 30°, the other is:
questions.explanation: Exterior angle = Sum of interior opposites. 110° - 30° = 80°.

questions.text: A triangle can have two right angles.
questions.explanation: If two angles were 90°, the sum would already be 180°, leaving 0° for the third angle, which is impossible.

questions.text: The difference between the lengths of any two sides of a triangle is ________ the third side.
questions.explanation: A property derived from the Triangle Inequality Property.

questions.text: Each angle of an equilateral triangle measures:
The altitude of a triangle always lies in the interior of the triangle.
questions.explanation: In an obtuse-angled triangle, altitudes from the acute vertices lie in the exterior.

questions.text: In an isosceles triangle, if the vertex angle is 80°, find the base angles.
Which of the following can be the sides of a right triangle?
questions.explanation: 6² + 8² = 36 + 64 = 100 = 10². It satisfies Pythagoras property.

questions.text: The longest side of a right triangle is the:
questions.explanation: The hypotenuse is always longer than either of the other two sides.

questions.text: If a triangle has angles 90°, 45°, 45°, it is a:
questions.explanation: It has one right angle and two equal angles/sides.

questions.text: Find the value of x if angles are x, 2x, and 3x.
A median of a triangle connects a vertex to the ________ of the opposite side.
questions.explanation: Definition of a median.

questions.text: In a triangle, if one angle is obtuse, the other two must be:
questions.explanation: The sum of angles is 180°. If one is > 90°, the sum of the others must be < 90°.

questions.text: The length of the third side of a triangle whose two sides are 6 cm and 8 cm must be between:
questions.explanation: Difference (8-6=2) < side < Sum (8+6=14).

questions.text: An equilateral triangle can also be called an isosceles triangle.
questions.explanation: An equilateral triangle has at least two equal sides, satisfying the isosceles condition.

questions.text: The point where all three medians of a triangle meet is called the:
questions.explanation: While not heavily emphasized in Grade 7, the Centroid is the intersection of medians.

questions.text: The point where all three altitudes meet is the:
questions.explanation: Orthocentre is the intersection point of altitudes.

questions.text: In an isosceles triangle, if the equal angles are 45° each, the third angle is:
A ladder 15 m long reaches a window 12 m high from the ground. Find the distance of the foot of the ladder from the wall.
A tree is broken at a height of 5 m and its top touches the ground at 12 m from its base. Find the original height.
questions.explanation: Broken part = √(5² + 12²) = 13 m. Total height = 5 + 13 = 18 m.

questions.text: Find the perimeter of the rectangle whose length is 40 cm and a diagonal is 41 cm.
questions.explanation: Width = √(41² - 40²) = √81 = 9 cm. Perimeter = 2(40 + 9) = 98 cm.

questions.text: Diagonal of a rhombus measure 16 cm and 30 cm. Find its perimeter.
questions.explanation: Side = √(8² + 15²) = 17 cm. Perimeter = 4 * 17 = 68 cm.

questions.text: The sum of any two angles of a triangle is always greater than the third angle.
questions.explanation: This is true for sides, but not necessarily for angles (e.g., 100°, 40°, 40°).

questions.text: An equilateral triangle has ________ lines of symmetry.
questions.explanation: One through each vertex and midpoint of the opposite side.

questions.text: In triangle ABC, if AB = AC and angle B = 50°, then angle A is:
questions.explanation: Angle C = Angle B = 50°. Angle A = 180 - (50 + 50) = 80°.

questions.text: In a right triangle, can the hypotenuse be 10 cm if the other sides are 6 cm and 7 cm?
questions.explanation: 6² + 7² = 36 + 49 = 85. Since 10² = 100 and 85 ≠ 100, it's not a right triangle.

questions.text: How many altitudes does a triangle have?
questions.explanation: One from each vertex.

questions.text: The sum of the interior opposite angles is 120°. The exterior angle is:
questions.explanation: By exterior angle property, they are equal.
